results.title: Phase 4.3 Wave 3 Astro shell scaffold
results.content: Implemented Phase 4.3 Wave 3 without creating a commit. Added dashboard/web as an Astro plus Tailwind workspace with package.json, astro.config.mjs, tsconfig.json, shared layout, shell pages for setup/login/client list/client detail, global styles, and a typed admin API client aimed at /v1/admin/clients, /v1/admin/audit, and /v1/auth/token-exchange. Verified with scaffold tests plus existing dashboard/admin backend tests; did not run astro build because frontend dependencies were not installed in this turn.

results.title: Astro dashboard runtime import fix
results.content: Fixed the Astro admin console integration bug where inline page scripts imported ../lib/api/admin directly and the browser fetched /lib/api/admin as a raw module path, causing 404s. Moved setup, login, and clients page logic into bundled script entry files under src/dashboard/src/scripts and referenced them from Astro pages via ?url so Vite/Astro emits proper dashboard asset URLs. Backend API contracts did not need changes.

results.title: Astro dashboard promoted to /dashboard
results.content: Promoted the Astro admin console under src/dashboard to the primary UI surface. Minder now serves static Astro assets from /dashboard, keeps /console only as a compatibility redirect, and uses a single static clients page with Python fallback to clients/index.html for /dashboard/clients/{id}. Docker now uses a Node build stage and copies the bundle to /app/dashboard-dist so production stays one image and one port. Browser/admin tests were updated to use Astro-shell semantics with seeded dist fixtures instead of the old server-rendered HTML assumptions.

results.title: Astro dashboard CSS path fix
results.content: The Astro admin console was missing base=/dashboard in src/dashboard/astro.config.mjs, so built CSS and JS assets resolved to root /_astro paths and failed when served by the Python app under /dashboard. Setting base to /dashboard fixes asset URLs; the dashboard bundle or Docker image must be rebuilt after the change because existing dist output still contains old asset paths.

results.title: Dashboard integration flow guarded by server state
results.content: The Astro dashboard cannot be treated as a blind static site for admin flows. The correct integration is stateful at the Python layer: /dashboard redirects to /dashboard/setup when no admin exists, to /dashboard/login when admins exist but there is no valid session, and to /dashboard/clients when an admin session is present. Static asset requests under /dashboard must bypass those auth redirects. Frontend JSON admin calls should also redirect 401 responses back to login/setup so the browser flow stays coherent.
